purpose: Single function `handleApiRoute(req, res, url)` containing every REST API endpoint handler for the dashboard server, dispatched as a sequential cascade of `if (url.pathname === ... && req.method === ...)` branches. Returns `true` if a route was matched and a response sent, `false` to let the caller fall through to 404. Endpoints cover: dashboard CRUD (`GET/POST /api/dashboards`, `DELETE /api/dashboards/:id`), per-dashboard data reads (`/initialization`, `/logs`, `/progress`, `/dispatchable`, `/metrics`, `/export`), per-dashboard mutations (`POST /archive`, `POST /save-history`, `POST /clear`), aggregate views (`/api/dashboards/statuses`, `/api/overview`), archive listing/reading/deletion, history list + analytics, and queue list + per-item read. Path parameters are validated through `sanitizePathParam` (max 100 chars, no `..`, no slashes/backslashes, must match `^[a-zA-Z0-9_][a-zA-Z0-9_.\-]*$`) before any filesystem operation â€” a path-traversal hard stop. The status-derivation logic is duplicated between `/api/dashboards/statuses` and `/api/overview` (allDone + hasFailed + hasInProgress booleans + `totalTasks > progressValues.length`). Mutating endpoints (archive, clear, save-history) are intentionally synchronous (`fs.writeFileSync`, `copyDirSync`) for atomicity within the request lifecycle.
gotchas: Route ORDER MATTERS â€” `DELETE /api/dashboards/:id` would also match the `:id` slot for reserved names like `statuses` if `sanitizePathParam` allowed them; `/api/dashboards/statuses` MUST be registered before any `:id` regex match attempt or the route would be shadowed.
`sanitizePathParam` is the ONLY guard against path traversal â€” every fs operation that consumes a URL parameter (id, name, queueId) must pass it through this function first. Do not bypass.
Status derivation is DUPLICATED between `/api/dashboards/statuses` and `/api/overview` â€” keep them in sync when changing logic, or extract to a shared helper.

`POST /api/dashboards/:id/clear` synchronously archives + writes history + truncates files â€” if any step fails after the archive copy, the dashboard is left half-cleared. The 500 path returns early before the truncation.

Archive `name` and history `task_name` are sanitized differently â€” archive uses raw task name in `${today}_${taskName}`, while save-history sanitizes via `replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/g, '_')`. The unsanitized archive path can fail on tasks with `/` or other shell-unsafe chars.
`buildHistorySummary` is called inside `/clear` and `/save-history`; it reads files synchronously â€” if files are mid-write by a worker the summary may be stale.

All async branches use IIFEs `(async () => {...})()` â€” they swallow rejections silently UNLESS wrapped in try/catch. The `/export` handler does this correctly; some other branches (e.g. `/initialization` async read) do not.
Returning `true` is mandatory after sending a response â€” forgetting it would cause double-responses when subsequent route checks also match.
patterns: Cascade dispatch â€” every route is a sequential if/return-true; no router lib.
Path traversal defense via `sanitizePathParam`: rejects `..`, `/`, `\`, and anything outside `[a-zA-Z0-9_.\-]` (must start with alphanumeric or underscore), max 100 chars.
`parseDashboardRoute(pathname)` regex `^/api/dashboards/([^/]+)(?:/(.*))?$` captures id + optional subpath in one match, so all `/api/dashboards/:id/<subpath>` endpoints share the same validated id.
Async DB-style reads use `fs.promises` via IIFE; sync mutations use `fs.writeFileSync` for in-request atomicity.
`sendJSON(res, statusCode, data)` is the canonical JSON response helper â€” sets `Content-Type: application/json` and stringifies the payload.
Default fallbacks via `{ ...DEFAULT_INITIALIZATION }` and `{ ...DEFAULT_LOGS }` (spread to avoid shared-reference mutation) when reads return null.
conventions: Status is derived from `task.total_tasks > 0 && progressValues.length < totalTasks` â€” a swarm with declared tasks is `in_progress` until all progress files exist.
Error messages for 400 responses include the validation rule (`alphanumeric with hyphens, underscores, or dots (max 100 chars, no path traversal)`).

related: electron/ipc-handlers.js (mirror IPC handlers for Electron mode)
